situation: audit-2026-07-02-002 finding AS-EC-017 (priority 17, pending): autonomous-loop.sh --bg mode process group leak. Line 26 installs 'kill 0' trap at script top level, before --bg bootstrap branch. When user runs --bg, parent nohup & child then exit 0; if SIGINT/SIGTERM arrives before parent exits (or shell propagates trap to backgrounded child), kill 0 sends to entire PGID → freshly-spawned background child killed alongside parent. Foreground-only long-running process should own the cleanup trap, not the transient bootstrap parent.
action: Moved trap installation past the --bg if-block so only the actual long-running process (foreground caller OR re-executed --bg child) installs kill-0 cleanup. Bootstrap parent in --bg mode exits cleanly without trap. Added explanatory comment block referencing both L-001 and AS-EC-017. bash -n syntax check passed. opt-worktree.sh auto-judged direction-shift (autonomous-loop.sh is in public-interfaces.txt) and opened new worktree.
outcome_evidence: After commit to opt-engine-shift-1782938027 and push confirmed, re-read of audit-cycle-state.json shows AS-EC-017 already marked merged by prior parallel context via case-2026-07-02-056 on opt-engine-shift-1782937136 (different approach: _cleanup() with kill -- -$$ explicit negative PGID). This fix is functionally equivalent but redundant. Branch auto/opt-engine-shift-1782938027 exists on remote but should be rejected in favor of the earlier fix.
next_suggestion: 所有 18 个 derived_fixes 均有 fix committed（14 merged + 4 pending merge）。下轮应检查 4 个 pending（AS-EC-013/014/016/018）是否已人工合并；若全部 merged/rejected，则 audit-cycle-state.status→cycle-complete 触发新一轮全量审计。
